data.title: Trump's Trade Policies Pose Greater Risk To South Korea's Economy Than Domestic Martial Law Crisis, Says Central Bank Chief: 'A Lot More Uncertainty'
data.description: The governor of the Bank of Korea, Rhee Chang-yong, expressed that the South Korean economy is more threatened by the trade policies of Donald Trump than the current political crisis in the country.

data.title: Top 3 Risk Off Stocks That May Collapse This Month
Waymo Eyes Expansion Of Autonomous Rides To Users In Miami By 2026
data.description: Waymo said on Thursday that it is looking to open autonomous rides to people in Miami, Florida in 2026.

data.title: Stellantis Partners With Zeta Energy To Develop Lithium Sulfur Batteries For Future EVs
data.description: Automaker Stellantis NV said on Thursday that it has entered into an agreement with U.S.-based Zeta Energy Corp to develop cheaper lithium-sulfur batteries to be deployed in electric vehicles by 2030.

data.title: US Stocks Likely To Open In Red As Investors Remain Cautious Ahead Of Key Payrolls Data: Bitcoin's Rally Above $100K Is 'Precursor' To S&P 500's Performance For Rest Of The Year, Fundstrat's Tom Lee Says
data.description: U.S. stocks could open in the red on Friday as investors exercise caution and take a break after pushing the Dow past 45,000 for the first time.
